title: Elatostema crispulihirtellum
what_description: Elatostema crispulihirtellum is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to China. It is a perennial herb with erect stems that grow up to 40 cm in height, and have a glabrous to sparsely hirtellous leaf surface. The leaves are ovate to elliptic in shape, measuring 5-15 cm in length, and 2.5-6.5 cm in width, with a serrated margin and a petiole 1-3 cm in length. The plant produces small, greenish-yellow flowers in an axillary panicle, and the fruit is an achene with a length of about 1 mm. Elatostema crispulihirtellum typically thrives in shaded, humid environments.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Elatostema crispulihirtellum are inflammation, which reduces with its anti-inflammatory properties, hypertension, which is managed through its vasodilatory effect, diabetes, which is treated by regulating blood sugar levels, fever, which is reduced by its antipyretic properties, and digestive issues, such as gastritis and dyspepsia, which are alleviated by its gastroprotective properties. Furthermore, cancer is inhibited by its antiproliferative and anti-inflammatory actions, skin irritation and skin inflammation are soothed by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, gastritis and gastroenteritis are treated by its gastroprotective properties, and wound healing is accelerated by its antioxidant and antimicrobial actions. Elatostema crispulihirtellum also helps in the management of asthma by reducing inflammation and regulating the immune response, and it alleviates migraine by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Finally, skin irritation and bacterial infections, such as diarrhea, are also treated by its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Elatostema crispulihirtellum are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which can protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. This plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Its antimicrobial action has been studied, showing potential in treating infections caused by bacteria and other microorganisms. Additionally, Elatostema crispulihirtellum has been found to exhibit antifungal properties, suggesting its use in treating fungal infections and diseases. The analgesic properties of this plant, likely due to its bioactive compounds, may provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with various conditions.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Elatostema crispulihirtellum are flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its potential therapeutic benefits. Additionally, the presence of phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, provides further antioxidant effects. Cardiac glycosides, also known as cardenolides, have been identified in Elatostema crispulihirtellum, and have been reported to have a potential role in cardiovascular health. These compounds may exert their effects by influencing ion transport and cardiac contractility. The combined action of these constituents could contribute to the plant's potential anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular benefits.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Elatostema crispulihirtellum are derived from its leaves, which contain various bioactive compounds. Elatostema tea is prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water, typically used for its anti-inflammatory properties. Elatostema infusion is similar, but it involves steeping the leaves in cold water for an extended period, often used to treat digestive issues. Elatostema tincture is a concentrated liquid extract made by soaking the leaves in a solvent, commonly used as a remedy for fever and pain. Additionally, decoction and elatostema salve preparations have also been documented, with the former involving boiling the leaves in water to release their medicinal properties and the latter being a topical application made from the leaves' oil.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Elatostema crispulihirtellum improperly include inducing nausea due to its high saponin content, which can also cause diarrhea in some individuals as their bodies struggle to process these compounds. Allergic reactions may occur in people who are sensitive to saponins or other compounds found in this plant, leading to symptoms such as skin irritation and potentially life-threatening reactions like anaphylaxis. Ingesting large amounts of Elatostema crispulihirtellum can also induce vomiting as the body attempts to expel the toxic substances. Skin irritation can be caused by direct contact with the plant's sap, which contains irritant compounds that can cause inflammation and discomfort. Furthermore, the improper use of Elatostema crispulihirtellum may exacerbate underlying health conditions, particularly those related to the digestive system, due to its toxic properties.

taxonomy: Elatostema crispulihirtellum is classified within the family Urticaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the nettle family. This classification places Elatostema crispulihirtellum among the nettle-like plants. The genus Elatostema is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. Elatostema crispulihirtellum, like other members of its genus, is a type of perennial herb. The classification of Elatostema crispulihirtellum is still being studied and refined by botanists.
